data.title: India's RBI proposes limits for banks' capital market exposure, acquisition funding
data.description:
The Reserve Bank of India released a draft circular on Friday, proposing that banks' total direct capital market and acquisition finance exposures must not exceed 20% of their tier 1 capital.

The RBI also proposed that the aggregate capital market exposure of banks to not exceed 40% of their tier 1 capital.

Tier 1 capital, the highest-quality capital of a bank, includes equity, retained earnings, and certain instruments that can absorb losses.

Earlier this month, the regulator allowed banks to fund acquisitions and raised the cap on loans for buying shares at initial public offerings, as part of a raft of measures to boost bank lending in the world's fifth-largest economy.

RBI proposed that the aggregate exposure of a bank towards acquisition finance shall not exceed 10% of its tier 1 capital.

In its proposed rules for acquisition finance, it said that banks may finance a maximum of 70% of the deal value, with at least 30% to be funded by the acquiring company.

It added that commercial banks can offer acquisition finance only to listed entities that have a satisfactory net worth and been profitable for the last three years.

data.title: Major shareholder notification
data.description:
OSLO, Norway - October 24, 2025 - Agilyx ASA (OSE: AGLX: OTCQX: AGXXF: ISIN:NO0010872468: WKN: A2QGQ) ("Agilyx" or the "Company") announces CirCap MasterIS.à.r.l ("CirCap Master") has today transferred all its 7,029,469 shares inAgilyx ASA (the "Company") that it received as consideration in relation totheclosing of the GreenDot Global share acquisition as announced by the Companyon15 October 2025, to its parent company, Circap Funds SCA SICAV RAIF ("CirCapFunds"). Following completion of the transaction, CirCap Master no longerholdsany shares in the Company, and Circap Funds owns 7,029,469 shares in theCompany, corresponding to an ownership interest of approximately 5.61% of theCompany's 125,352,758 outstanding shares and votes. The shares remain subjecttoa lock-up undertaking expiring 10 January 2026, followed by a 90-day windowduring which any sale of the consideration shares received by the sellers intheGreenDot Global acquisition will be capped at 20% of the prior 30-day averagedaily trading volume in the Company's shares.

This information is subject to the disclosure requirements pursuant tosections4-2 and 5 -12 of the Norwegian Securities Trading Act.

ABOUT AGILYXAgilyx ASA is a leading global investment platform supporting the developmentofplastic waste feedstock supply to the recycling industry. Through its 44%stakein GreenDot Global, Europe's largest waste plastic recycling platform,generating over EUR 400 million in annual revenues, Agilyx gains access tolargevolumes of post-use plastic and advanced sorting and recycling infrastructureinGermany, Austria, and Italy, helping supply critical European-sourcedfeedstockto the European advanced recycling markets. Through Cyclyx, its (50%) jointventure with ExxonMobil (25%) and LyondellBasell (25%), Agilyx supports thecollection and processing of post-use plastic waste into custom-formulated,high-quality feedstock solutions for global plastic producers. Agilyx marketsStyrenyx, its proprietary advanced recycling technology, to recyclepolystyrenewaste into recycled styrene monomers for reuse in new, high-quality products.Byenabling the shift from a linear "make-take-waste" model to a circulareconomy,Agilyx helps solve the crisis of plastic waste and supports the globaltransition to a low-carbon future.

Inquiries: investors@agilyx.comLearn more at: www.agilyx.comFor media resources, visit: https://www.agilyx.com/media-room/

data.title: Colombian man sentenced to 42 years for murder and dismemberment of UK couple
data.description:
A Colombian national who murdered two men in west London and transported parts of their dismembered bodies in suitcases to southwest England was sentenced on Friday to 42 years in prison.

Yostin Andres Mosquera, 35, was convicted in July of killing Albert Alfonso, 62, and Paul Longworth, 71, at their flat in July 2024. Prosecutors said Mosquera had bludgeoned Longworth with a hammer and stabbed Alfonso to death after the pair had sex.

He then decapitated and dismembered both victims, placing parts of their bodies into suitcases and travelling to Bristol, southwest England, where he abandoned the luggage on the Clifton Suspension Bridge.

He had pleaded not guilty to two counts of murder but admitted to the manslaughter of Alfonso, claiming he had lost control after Alfonso killed Longworth and threatened his family. The jury rejected his defence and found him guilty of both murders.

On Friday, Mosquera pleaded guilty to three new charges of possessing indecent images of children.

Judge Joel Bennathan, who described the murders as "thoroughly wicked crimes," handed down his sentence of life imprisonment with a minimum term of 42 years at Woolwich Crown Court on Friday.

"It was their tragedy that you, Yostin Mosquera, came into their lives and, on 11 July last year, killed them both," Bennathan said.

"I accept there was an economic imbalance, which was the reason you committed these murders, but that does not amount to any sort of mitigation."

data.title: GSK Wins FDA Approval for Blood-Cancer Drug Blenrep — Update
data.description:
By Adria Calatayud

GSK won approval from the U.S. Food and Drug Administration for blood-cancer drug Blenrep, paving the way for a return to the market of a medicine the company sees as a potential blockbuster.

The FDA nod comes after an advisory committee voted against approval of Blenrep in July, which cast doubt on whether GSK would be able to reintroduce the drug in the U.S. Drug regulators gave accelerated approval to Blenrep in 2020, but later revoked the decision when a clinical trial showed the drug--then used alone--didn't outperform an existing treatment.

The FDA approved Blenrep as part of a drug combination for adult patients with relapsed or refractory multiple myeloma who have received at least two prior lines of therapy. It also said the drug would be available only through a risk evaluation and mitigation strategy, a program that restricts how medicines can be marketed to enhance safety.

Shares in GSK fell as much as 3% before recovering somewhat, trading down 1.2% in European afternoon Friday.

Blenrep's approval had been in doubt after the advisory committee vote and the FDA's decision to allow the drug to return to the U.S. market is positive for GSK, but a highly limited label likely reduces the commercial opportunity, analysts at UBS wrote in a note to clients.

GSK, which has previously estimated the drug's annual peak sales could top 3 billion pounds ($4 billion), said it continues to work on a clinical program to demonstrate Blenrep's potential benefits in earlier lines of treatment.

The company's chief scientific officer, Tony Wood, said Blenrep fulfills a major patient need and that it can redefine treatment for multiple myeloma patients.

The approval was based on results of a late-stage clinical trial that showed a reduction in the risk of death and tripled median progression-free survival, the company said.

GSK said the U.S. approval for Blenrep follows prior authorizations in the European Union, the U.K., Japan, Canada, Switzerland and Brazil.

data.title: QleanAir launches FS 70 Welding - A powerful mobile air cleaner developed for welding and grinding applications
data.description:
QleanAir AB (publ.), a premium provider of indoor air cleaning solutions, introduces the FS 70 Welding - a powerful, energy-efficient air cleaner designed to minimize the levels of welding fumes and grinding dust in metalworking environments.

Developed through extensive research and in close collaboration with the industry, the FS 70 Welding features multi-stage mechanical filters with high fire classification, ensuring safe, efficient, and cost-effective operation. Its unique filter configuration and modular flexibility address the specific challenges faced by metalworking businesses in their daily operations.

By continuously recirculating and filtering air throughout the workspace, it protects employees and improves air quality considerably in production halls. This makes it ideal where source capture isn’t possible or as a complement to existing extraction systems.

Key features of the QleanAir FS 70 Welding:

  • Unique filter configuration: Multi-stage mechanical filter solution with high fire classification for safe, efficient, and cost-effective operation.
  • High airflow: Powerful air cleaning for large spaces with constant airflow regulation.
  • Standalone design: Operates independently of HVAC systems.
  • Flexible and mobile: Plug-and-play setup with robust wheels and accessory options.
  • Quiet performance: Low noise levels (30–63 dB(A)).

“Our goal was to develop a flexible solution tailored to the specific conditions of welding and grinding processes in the metalworking industry,” says Johan Billing, Product Manager at QleanAir Scandinavia. “FS 70 Welding is based on a unique filter configuration and offers modular flexibility and efficient air cleaning throughout the entire facility, providing an effective alternative when source capture is not possible or as a complement to existing extraction systems.”

QleanAir develops flexible air cleaning solutions adaptable to customer needs in the industry. The FS 70 Welding is now launched across Europe.

For more information, visit https://www.qleanair.com.
